nodes.name: Bycanistes
nodes.summary: Bycanistes（Bycanistes）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联6种鸟类，如Silvery-cheeked hornbill、Black-and-white-casqued hornbill、Brown-cheeked hornbill等6种鸟类。

nodes.name: Silvery-cheeked hornbill
nodes.summary: Silvery-cheeked hornbill（Bycanistes brevis）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联1种鸟类，如Silvery-cheeked hornbill。

nodes.name: Silvery-cheeked hornbill
nodes.summary: The silvery-cheeked hornbill (Bycanistes brevis) is a large species of hornbill found in Africa. Silvery-cheeked hornbills are residents of the tall evergreen forests of East Africa from Ethiopia to South Africa. In Zimbabwe it is threatened by habitat destruction and its presence in South Africa is marginal, but it remains locally fairly common, especially in the northern and central parts of its range. Description It measures 75 to 80 centimetres (30 to 31 in) in length, and has a very large cream-colored casque on the beak. The head is silver-grey and the rest of the plumage is iridescent black, except for the white rump, lower back, thighs, vent and tip of the outer tail-feathers. The sexes are similar except the female has a smaller casque and reddish skin around the eyes. Diet It is omnivorous, feeding on fruits, insects, small birds, rodents, small reptiles and centipedes. Behaviour Usually they live in pairs and sometimes roost in flocks of hundreds of individuals. They breed in spring (September and October; at least in part of its range) and lay clutches of one to three white eggs, incubated for 40 days. The young remain with both parents for about 80 days. Gallery References BirdLife Species Factsheet. External links Silvery-cheeked hornbill - Species text in The Atlas of Southern African Birds.
